content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
July 7,
1992
Paula Kimbrell
Assistant City Attorney
City of Oxnard
300
W. Third Street
Oakland,
CA
93030
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
1-92-234
Dear Ms.
Kimbrell:
As the Assistant City Attorney for the City of Oxnard,
you
have requested advice concerning the conflict—of—interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”)J/
You have asked if an unidentified city councilmember should
have disqualified himself in August of 1990 from participating in
a decision to designate a specific area a greenbelt area.
You
further asked if the councilmember should have disqualified
himself,
is the decision of the city council invalid.
Since you are requesting advice on past conduct,
the
Commission is declining to give informal assistance.
(Regulation
18329(c)(4).)
I have enclosed
a copy of Commission Regulation
18702.3 which discusses the material financial effect on real
property which is indirectly involved in a decision and a copy of
the Act.
Perhaps this regulation will assist you in the future.
If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322—5901.
Sincerely,
Jeánette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division
JET/jt
Enclosures
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

sections.question: You have asked if an unidentified city councilmember should have disqualified himself in August of 1990 from participating in a decision to designate a specific area a greenbelt area. You further asked if the councilmember should have disqualified himself, is the decision of the city council invalid.
sections.conclusion: Since you are requesting advice on past conduct, the Commission is declining to give informal assistance. (Regulation 18329(c)(4).)
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city councilmember should have disqualified themselves from a 1990 greenbelt designation decision under the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions, and whether the council's decision would be invalid if disqualification was required.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide informal advice because the request concerns past conduct, citing Regulation 18329(c)(4). The FPPC provided relevant regulations for future reference.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Document addresses conflict-of-interest question under Political Reform Act but explicitly declines to advise on past conduct per regulation.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have asked if an unidentified city councilmember should have disqualified himself in August of 1990 from participating in a decision to designate a specific area a greenbelt area. You further asked if the councilmember should have disqualified himself, is the decision of the city council invalid.

CONCLUSION: Since you are requesting advice on past conduct, the Commission is declining to give informal assistance. (Regulation 18329(c)(4).)
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission July 7, 1992 Paula Kimbrell Assistant City Attorney City of Oxnard 300 W. Third Street Oakland, CA 93030 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. 1-92-234 Dear Ms. Kimbrell: As the Assistant City Attorney for the City of Oxnard, you have requested advice concerning the conflict—of—interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”)J/ You have asked if an unidentified city councilmember should have disqualified himself in August of 1990 from participating in a decision to designate a specific area a greenbelt area. You further asked if the councilmember should have disqualified himself, is the decision of the city council invalid. Since you are requesting advice on past conduct, the Commission is declining to give informal assistance. (Regulation 18329(c)(4).) I have enclosed a copy of Commission Regulation 18702.3 which discusses the material financial effect on real property which is indirectly involved in a decision and a copy of the Act. Perhaps this regulation will assist you in the future. If you have any questions, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322—5901. Sincerely, Jeánette E. Turvill Political Reform Consultant Legal Division JET/jt Enclosures 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660
embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide informal advice to the City of Oxnard's Assistant City Attorney regarding whether a councilmember should have disqualified themselves from a 1990 greenbelt decision, because the request concerns past conduct. The FPPC enclosed relevant regulations for future guidance.
